body.title: [sprint] Retire the weekly/monthly audit cadence; fold eval-run + weekly-retro + audit-expert into daily day-close
body.intention: I intend that the day-close ritual has NO weekly-gated or monthly-gated branch — the audit/eval/retro work that lived on the Friday/first-Monday cadence is absorbed into the daily flow, so coverage is continuous and no single day carries a heavy batch.
body.description:
Akien 2026-07-03: 'weekly was supposed to go away and be wrapped into daily.' Today day-close step 13.5 gates three heavyweight skills behind `date +%u = 5` (Friday: /eval-run + /weekly-retro + /audit-expert) and a first-Monday-of-month branch (/audit-expert --mode=monthly). That weekly cadence is being retired.

**Q1 (design fork — needs Akien): what shape does 'wrapped into daily' take?**
  (a) RECOMMENDED — rotate a slice per weekday: each day runs one dimension (e.g. Mon=eval-run, Tue=retro delta, Wed/Thu/Fri=2-3 experts) so a full week still covers what the weekly did, but no heavy day and daily cost stays bounded.
  (b) Run all three every day (full coverage daily; ~5-7x the audit cost — likely too heavy).
  (c) Lighter daily variants of each (a fast eval subset, a 1-line retro delta, 1 random expert/day) that together approximate the weekly over a week.
A1 (Akien 2026-07-03): **(a)** — rotate a slice per weekday ('progressively across the week'). A full week still covers what the weekly did; no heavy day; daily cost stays bounded. Build the (a) fold.

**What to build (once Q1 answered):**
- Remove step 13.5 (Friday block) and the first-Monday monthly block from skills/day-close/SKILL.md.
- Fold the chosen daily form into the day-close steps (new step, or inline into the audit step 4/5).
- Reconcile with the audit-emphasis/telemetry substrate if the daily form reads it.
- Ensure /eval-run, /weekly-retro (rename? it's no longer weekly), /audit-expert still work when invoked directly (don't delete the skills, just change WHO calls them and WHEN).

**Affected files:** skills/day-close/SKILL.md (remove weekly/monthly gates, add daily fold), possibly skills/weekly-retro/ (rename to retro or daily-retro), skills/eval-run/ + skills/audit-expert/ (mode params for a daily slice)
**Design rules:** none apply (skill-cadence change); audit-feedback check on touched skills/
**Scope boundary:** IN: retire weekly+monthly gating in day-close, fold into daily per Q1. OUT: deleting the eval-run/audit-expert/weekly-retro skills themselves (they stay as callable units); changing WHAT each audit checks.
**Test plan:** no automated tests because: this is a skill-prose/cadence change (skills are markdown rituals, not code). Verification is a dry-run of day-close on a non-Friday showing the daily audit slice runs, and on a Friday showing NO special heavy batch. State this dry-run as the acceptance check.
**Validation (runtime):** a day-close on any weekday runs its audit slice (a log/slate line naming which dimension ran); no day-of-week branch remains in SKILL.md (grep 'date +%u' returns nothing in day-close).
**Observable via:** slate 'Done today' notes the daily audit slice that ran; `grep -c 'date +%u' skills/day-close/SKILL.md` == 0 after.
**Rollback:** git revert restores the weekly/monthly gates.

## Pre-computed constraints (auto-stamped at add time)
[hard_block] ⛔ NO SQLITE. EVER. POSTGRES OR FLAT-FILE ONLY. See banner at top of this file. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[hard_block] Log every state change and every interface crossing. State changes: ticket status — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] No TheIgors imports. UnseenUniversity must be portable without TheIgors present. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] No live keys or passwords in source. `.env` is gitignored. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] `pip install -e .` must succeed at all times (even with empty stubs). — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] Canonical dev-process memory — one home, no exceptions. Every dev-process artifact — — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] device.py and shim.py are the design center. Every component inherits from — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] bus/ owns comms:// routing. Nothing outside bus/ speaks to IMAP directly. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] skeleton/ owns the MCP aggregator and flat-file registry. No Postgres dependency. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] devices/ contains one subdirectory per device; each is independently deployable. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] `~/.unseen_university/logs/<device>/<stream>/` is the canonical log hierarchy — — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] Every ticket closes proven, or declares itself unproven — no discriminator. A ticket that — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
…and 39 more (lower-severity or capped) — query constraints_get for the full set
